title: Ruellia helianthema
intro: Ruellia helianthema, also known as the "Mexican petunia," is a flowering plant species native to Mexico and Central America, belonging to the family Acanthaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which can aid in the treatment of various ailments such as skin infections and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Ruellia helianthema include flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Ruellia helianthema is often used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into topical creams and ointments to leverage its antibacterial and anti-inflammatory effects.

side_effects: Ruellia helianthema, commonly known as the Mexican Petunia, is a flowering plant that has gained popularity in landscaping due to its vibrant purple flowers. However, like many plants, it can cause some side effects when handled improperly. The sap of the Mexican Petunia can cause skin irritation, allergic reactions, and even eye problems in some individuals.
